overview: Refactor `viewnode ctx list` and `viewnode ns list` to share explicit list-entry preparation logic while preserving current command behavior, alphabetical output ordering, active-item semantics including `default` namespace fallback, existing error handling, and repository validation through `make test`.

userStories.title: Extract shared list-entry preparation helper
userStories.description: Introduce explicit list-entry structures and a narrow shared helper for ordered active-state preparation so the duplicated list-building behavior used by `ctx list` and `ns list` is centralized without changing command-specific data retrieval.
acceptanceCriteria: The codebase defines explicit list-entry structures for context and namespace display rows.
A shared helper prepares sorted entries from raw names plus one active name without performing kubeconfig or Kubernetes API reads.
Focused automated tests cover deterministic ordering and active-state marking for the shared helper.
userStories.notes: Keep the helper in the existing command support structure and avoid introducing a new parallel command abstraction.

userStories.title: Refactor context list to use shared entries
userStories.description: Update `viewnode ctx list` to build and render context output through the shared list-entry path while preserving current output format, active-context selection, and error behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: Running `viewnode ctx list` still prints contexts in alphabetical order using the existing `[marker] name` format.
Only the kubeconfig current context is marked with `[*]`.
If kubeconfig loading fails, the command still returns a descriptive error and produces no partial list output.
Automated tests cover successful context listing and at least one kubeconfig failure case.
userStories.notes: Preserve the current raw kubeconfig lookup path in `cmd/ctx/list.go`; only the list preparation should change.

userStories.title: Refactor namespace list with default fallback semantics
userStories.description: Update `viewnode ns list` to use the shared list-entry path while keeping the existing namespace query flow and normalizing an empty active kubeconfig namespace to `default` before active-state comparison.
acceptanceCriteria: Running `viewnode ns list` still prints namespaces in alphabetical order using the existing `[marker] name` format.
If the active kubeconfig context has no explicit namespace, `default` is treated as the active namespace for marker comparison.
If namespace discovery fails, the command still returns a descriptive error and produces no partial list output.
Automated tests cover successful namespace listing, `default` fallback behavior, and at least one namespace lookup failure case.
userStories.notes: Keep namespace discovery tied to the existing Kubernetes API call path and do not add extra cluster requests.

userStories.title: Run full validation for list-command refactor
userStories.description: Finish the refactor by verifying command-level regressions are covered and the repository test suite passes with the new shared list-entry implementation.
acceptanceCriteria: Command-level tests cover context listing success, namespace listing success, namespace `default` fallback, and failure propagation for both affected commands.
The final implementation still follows the established `cmd/ctx`, `cmd/ns`, and `cmd/config` repository structure.
Running `make test` succeeds after the refactor changes are complete.
userStories.notes: This story is the final verification slice for the refactor and should run after the command-specific stories land.
